QF.id: VII Market Participants in Securities Markets_15

question.text: Which of the following correctly describes 'Pay-In' in the context of clearing corporations?

answer.text: The process whereby a clearing member brings money and/or securities to the clearing corporation — the first phase of settlement
The process of distributing dividends to shareholders
The process of returning margin deposits to brokers
The process of collecting listing fees from companies

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Pay-In is the first phase of settlement where clearing members and custodians bring money and/or securities to the Clearing House/Corporation. Pay-Out is the second phase where the clearing corporation pays money or delivers securities to the clearing members and custodians. **The Trap:** Students may reverse Pay-In and Pay-Out. Remember: Pay-In = members pay INTO the clearing corporation. Pay-Out = clearing corporation pays OUT to members. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series V and VI cover Pay-In/Pay-Out timelines and processes in detail. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Settlement = two phases: Pay-In (members → CC) then Pay-Out (CC → members). In T+1 settlement, pay-in of funds and securities happens on T+1, and pay-out follows on the same day.
